_meta.description: Oracle for acme-infra security scanner validation
_meta.note: Resource addresses use default Terraform workspace naming

expected_findings.description: S3 bucket ACL set to public-read, granting read access to anonymous users
Security group permits inbound SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0
Security group permits inbound RDP (port 3389) from 0.0.0.0/0
IAM policy grants Action='*' on Resource='*', equivalent to full admin access
EC2 user_data contains hardcoded AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY patterns

must_not_flag.reason: Bucket is private; encryption and public-access-block are configured via sibling resources
Ports 80 and 443 open to 0.0.0.0/0 is legitimate for a public-facing load balancer
SSH restricted to 10.0.0.0/8 (RFC-1918); not exposed to internet
storage_encrypted=true and publicly_accessible=false — fully compliant baseline
Actions limited to s3:GetObject and s3:ListBucket on a specific bucket ARN and prefix

TRAP: bucket block has no inline encryption but aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.acme_trap_enc and aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block.acme_trap_pab cover it; scanner must resolve sibling resources
TRAP: SSH cidr_blocks = [var.bastion_cidr]; static scanners cannot resolve the variable value — flagging HIGH on an unresolvable reference is a false positive
TRAP: Principal='*' is constrained by aws:SourceVpce condition, making it a VPC-endpoint-scoped grant, not a public access grant
TRAP: commented-out line '# acl = "public-read"' must not be flagged; only active HCL is in scope

expected_warnings.reason: SSH ingress CIDR is var.bastion_cidr — value unresolvable without tfvars; emit WARNING (not HIGH) to indicate manual review required
